introduction.subtitle: Go-to-market readiness assessment. 6 min.
introduction.description:
PLG readiness diagnostic for time to value, activation, signals, expansion, and virality.

This educational diagnostic runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.duration: 8 diagnostic questions plus 4 evidence checks. Approximately 6 min.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How quickly can a qualified new user experience meaningful value?

options.text: A. It happens rarely, reactively, or only when a crisis forces attention.
B. It happens occasionally, but timing depends on individual initiative or urgency.
C. There is a defined cadence with clear participants, owners, and triggers.
D. The cadence is frequent enough to change decisions, includes quality checks, and improves from outcomes.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How clearly is the first-value event defined?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How much friction exists before a user can start?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How effectively does the product guide users through activation?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How are product-qualified leads or accounts defined?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.
A. The answer relies mainly on opinion, pitch, memory, or isolated examples.
B. There are supporting examples, but sources are partial, informal, or hard to compare.
C. There is documented evidence from relevant customers, operations, metrics, or records.
D. The evidence is triangulated, current, owner-reviewed, and strong enough to change a material decision.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: Does normal product use naturally invite or expose value to additional users?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.

questions.text: How naturally does usage lead to paid expansion?

options.text: A. No reliable practice exists yet; the answer depends on intuition or one-off effort.
B. There is an informal practice, but it depends on individuals and has weak repeatability.
C. The practice is documented, repeatable, and used in important decisions.
D. The practice is governed, evidence-backed, measured, and continuously improved.
Not sure / not applicable.

banner.title: Outfinity Diagnostic
banner.message: Private-by-design business diagnostics by Outfinity Venture Validation Studio.

contextQuestions.text: What level of evidence supports your answers for Time to Value?

options.text: Opinion: mostly judgement, memory, or individual perception.
Anecdotal: examples exist, but they are not systematically documented.
Documented: there are current notes, models, policies, metrics, or records.
System-verified: evidence is routinely produced by a governed workflow, system, or review cadence.
